ORDER DENYING SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT (CCP 425.16)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BING JUAN CAO, AND JIE FRANG LIAO SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO SHOW THAT THE GRAVAMEN OF THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITIES. DEFENDANTS MAY BRING A MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES REGARDING THE 15TH AND 16TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION AROSE FROM PETITIONING ACTIVITY AND PLAINTIFFS CANNOT AVOID LIABILITY FOR FEES BY DISMISSING THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE THEIR RESPONSIVE PLEADING TO THE COMPLAINT WITHIN 25 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-20-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BING JUAN CAO, and, JIE FRANG LIAO SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Appearances: John N. Gulick, Jr. Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 782-1330 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Robert Chan, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 759-8877 appearing for Plaintiffs; Joshua N. Rosen, Esq. of Bledsoe (415) 981-5411 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Defendants fail to show that the gravamen of the remaining causes of action arise from petitioning activities. Defendants may bring a motion for attorney fees regarding the 15th and 16th causes of action. Those causes of action arose from petitioning activity and plaintiffs cannot avoid liability for fees by dismissing those causes of action. Defendants shall file their responsive pleading to the complaint within 25 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

PROOF OF SERVICE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2010 Off Calendar
